original_hypothesis.title: stepDuration=0 in StepVesting permanently locks all deposited tokens
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When a token issuer deploys a vesting plan via VestingDeployer.deployVesting() where numOfSteps > (endTime - startTime), the constructor computes stepDuration = (endTime - startTime) / numOfSteps = 0 via integer truncation. All subsequent calls to claimable() divide elapsedTime by stepDuration (0), causing a permanent revert. No beneficiary can ever claim tokens from this vesting plan.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a code defect in the deployment path: deployVesting validates startTime < endTime and steps > 0, but never validates that stepDuration > 0. Any tokens deposited via createVesting into this plan are permanently locked with no recovery mechanism.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Constructor: stepDuration = (_endTime - _startTime) / _numOfSteps; claimable(): currentStep = elapsedTime / stepDuration — division by zero when stepDuration=0

preconditions: Token issuer calls deployVesting with numOfSteps > (endTime - startTime)
At least one beneficiary has tokens created in this vesting plan

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: stepDuration = (_endTime - _startTime) / _numOfSteps truncates to 0 when steps exceed duration in seconds
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Accidental or intentional misconfiguration at deployment time
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: All tokens deposited into the vesting contract are permanently locked — total loss for all beneficiaries

economic_questions: Is there any mechanism to recover tokens from a broken vesting plan?
Can the VestingManager or VestingDeployer extract tokens from a non-claimable plan?
Does the marketplace listing flow also revert (transferVesting calls within the same plan)?
validation_requirements: Deploy vesting with steps=200, duration=100s and confirm stepDuration=0
Attempt claim after startTime and confirm revert
Confirm no admin rescue path exists

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Only gas costs to deploy vesting plan
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Direct profit for attacker is 0; total value at risk = all tokens deposited into this plan
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Griefing attack: unlimited damage relative to cost

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Total loss of all tokens allocated to the vesting plan

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All beneficiaries of the broken vesting plan
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Entire token allocation deposited into the vesting contract

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: stepDuration = (endTime-startTime) / numOfSteps; any case where result < 1 yields 0
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm division by zero in claimable() with stepDuration=0

validation_result.impact_summary: When numOfSteps exceeds (endTime - startTime), stepDuration truncates to 0, causing permanent division-by-zero revert in claimable()/claim(), permanently locking all deposited tokens with no recovery path.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The SecondSwap_StepVesting constructor computes stepDuration = (_endTime - _startTime) / _numOfSteps. When _numOfSteps > (_endTime - _startTime), Solidity integer division truncates to 0. The deployVesting function in VestingDeployer validates startTime < endTime and steps > 0, but never validates that the resulting stepDuration > 0. Once stepDuration is 0, the claimable() function performs `elapsedTime / stepDuration` which is division by zero and always reverts. Since claim() depends on claimable(), no beneficiary can ever claim tokens. The transferVesting function does not use stepDuration directly, so internal vesting transfers still work, but actual ERC20 tokens remain locked in the contract forever since there is no withdrawal/rescue mechanism.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The constructor at the last line of the constructor block sets `stepDuration = (_endTime - _startTime) / _numOfSteps;`. The claimable() function contains `uint256 currentStep = elapsedTime / stepDuration;` which will always revert when stepDuration=0 and currentTime >= startTime. The deployVesting function checks `startTime < endTime` and `steps > 0` but never checks that `(endTime - startTime) / steps > 0`. There is no admin rescue function in SecondSwap_StepVesting to extract tokens.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: When _numOfSteps > (_endTime - _startTime), integer division truncates to 0. No require statement validates the result is non-zero.

vulnerable_code_snippets.code_snippet:
uint256 elapsedTime = currentTime - startTime;
uint256 currentStep = elapsedTime / stepDuration;
vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Division by stepDuration=0 causes a permanent revert for any call after startTime, making tokens unclaimable forever.
Missing validation: require((endTime - startTime) / steps > 0). The function validates startTime < endTime and steps > 0 but does not validate that the computed stepDuration is non-zero.

calling_sequence: 1. Token owner calls deployVesting(tokenAddress, startTime=1000, endTime=1100, steps=200, vestingId) — duration=100, steps=200
2. deployVesting validates startTime < endTime (1000 < 1100 = true), steps > 0 (200 > 0 = true) — passes all checks
4. stepDuration immutable is set to 0 permanently
5. Token owner calls createVesting(beneficiary, 1000000) — tokens transferred into the contract via safeTransferFrom
6. After startTime, beneficiary calls claim() which calls claimable(msg.sender)
7. claimable(): currentTime >= startTime, so elapsedTime = currentTime - startTime > 0
8. currentStep = elapsedTime / stepDuration = elapsedTime / 0 → REVERT (division by zero)
9. claim() reverts permanently. No admin rescue function exists. Tokens locked forever.
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The path from deployVesting through the StepVesting constructor to claimable() shows that: (1) deployVesting permits any steps value > 0 without checking stepDuration, (2) the constructor silently sets stepDuration=0 via integer truncation, (3) claimable() divides by stepDuration which is 0, causing permanent revert. The _createVesting function and transferVesting do not use stepDuration so vestings can be created and transferred, but tokens remain locked since claim() is the only way to extract ERC20 tokens from the contract.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: DeployStepVestingAsOwner.ts uses default parameters startTime=1732517966, endTime=1732604366, numOfSteps=250 which gives duration=86400 and stepDuration=345 (safe). However, the vulnerability is in the validation logic of deployVesting which is called by any token owner at runtime, not just in deployment scripts.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: A token owner (or attacker who becomes one) calls deployVesting with parameters where steps > (endTime - startTime). For example: startTime=now, endTime=now+100, steps=200. This creates a vesting contract with stepDuration=0. The token owner then calls createVesting to deposit tokens for beneficiaries. Those tokens are permanently locked — claimable() and claim() always revert due to division by zero. There is no emergency withdrawal, no admin rescue, and the immutable stepDuration cannot be changed. This can occur accidentally (misconfiguration) or intentionally (griefing attack where attacker is also the token owner trapping their own or others' tokens).
validation_result.impact_assessment: CRITICAL: All tokens deposited via createVesting/createVestings into a vesting plan with stepDuration=0 are permanently and irrecoverably locked. No beneficiary can ever claim. The contract has no admin rescue function, no emergency withdrawal, and stepDuration is immutable. The total value at risk equals the entire token allocation deposited into the broken vesting contract.
recommended_mitigations: Add require(stepDuration > 0, 'SS_StepVesting: stepDuration must be > 0') in the StepVesting constructor after computing stepDuration
Add require((endTime - startTime) / steps > 0, 'SS_VestingDeployer: stepDuration would be zero') in deployVesting before creating the contract
Consider adding an emergency token rescue function callable only by tokenIssuer or admin for edge cases where the vesting contract is broken
Add a check in claimable() that handles stepDuration=0 gracefully (e.g., if stepDuration == 0 and block.timestamp >= startTime, treat all tokens as immediately claimable)
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy VestingDeployer and VestingManager with proper wiring. 2. Set token owner for a test ERC20. 3. Call deployVesting with startTime=block.timestamp, endTime=block.timestamp+100, steps=200. 4. Verify stepDuration=0 on the deployed StepVesting. 5. Approve tokens and call createVesting(beneficiary, 1e18). 6. Advance time past startTime. 7. Call claim() from beneficiary — expect revert. 8. Call claimable(beneficiary) — expect revert. 9. Verify no other function can extract the deposited ERC20 tokens.
uncertainties: Whether a token owner would realistically set numOfSteps > duration in seconds — this could happen with daily steps where duration is specified in seconds but steps are specified as days (e.g., 365 steps for a 30-day vesting expressed as 30*86400 seconds would be safe, but 365 steps for 300 seconds would not)
additional_testing_needed: Verify that transferVesting still works with stepDuration=0 (it should, since it doesn't use stepDuration)
Verify that marketplace listing/unlisting works with a broken vesting plan (the listVesting flow calls transferVesting which doesn't use stepDuration)
Check if any external tooling or frontend enforces stepDuration > 0 validation
